text: The mission of Jesus in Judea in John 3:22–36 has not been described in much detail in biblical scholarship. Related to this is the question about the “Jew” with whom the disciples of John the Baptist were at odds according to John 3:25. A look at the geographical setting of Jesus’ mission in John 2:13–4:54 can shed more light on these questions. Apparently, Jesus’ mission in this part of the Fourth Gospel follows the same geographical structure as indicated in Acts 1:8 where the disciples are appointed to be Jesus’ witnesses in Jerusalem, and in Judea and Samaria and to the very end of the earth. However, in John this mission leads Jesus to Galilee. All of God’s people are to hear his message and believe before the controversies with the Jewish leaders of John 5–10 take place. If Jesus’ mission to Israel includes his mission in Judea, the Ἰουδαῖος of John 3:25 must be translated as ‘Judean’. Jesus in Judea. Tot onlangs is nie veel aandag aan die geografiese agtergrond van Jesus se sending in Judea in Johannes 3:22–36 geskenk nie. Saam met hierdie gebrek aan belangstelling is die vraagstuk ten opsigte van die sogenaamde ‘Jood’ wat volgens Johannes 3:25 in ’n argument met die dissipels van Johannes die Doper betrokke was. ’ n Blik op die geografiese agtergrond van Jesus se sending volgens Johannes 2:13–4:54 kan meer lig op hierdie kwessies werp. Jesus se sending, soos in die Vierde Evangelie beskryf, volgklaarblyklik dieselfde geografiese struktuur soos dit in Handelinge 1:8 voorstel: Die dissipels sal Jesus se getuies in Jerusalem, die hele Judea en Samaria, en tot aan die uithoeke van die aarde wees. Volgens Johannes lei Jesus se sending Hom egter na Galilea waar al God se volgelinge na sy leringe luister en tot geloof kom voor die verskeie konfrontasies met die Joodse leiers sou plaasvind soos in Johannes 5–10 beskryf. Indien Jesus se sending in Judea deel vorm van sy sending in Israel, kan die Ἰουδαῖος, soos inJohannes 3:25 beskryf, eerder as ‘Judeër’ verstaan word.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
